title: [LoopUnrollPass] Indent `LLVM_DEBUG()` messages based on our depth in the `tryToUnrollLoop()` call graph
author: justinfargnoli
description:
Unify the ad-hoc use of whitespace in `LLVM_DEBUG()` messages. 

This approach should also make it easier to see which loop debug messages correspond to and which part of the loop unrolling heuristics each message corresponds to.

tests.test_body:
define i32 @full_unroll_profitability_analysis(ptr %A, ptr %B) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %result, %for.body ]
  %idxA = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %loadA = load i32, ptr %idxA, align 4
  %idxB = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %B, i32 %i
  %loadB = load i32, ptr %idxB, align 4
  %mul1 = mul i32 %loadA, %loadB
  %add1 = add i32 %mul1, %loadA
  %mul2 = mul i32 %add1, %loadB
  %sub1 = sub i32 %mul2, %loadA
  %add2 = add i32 %sub1, %loadB
  %mul3 = mul i32 %add2, %loadA
  %sub2 = sub i32 %mul3, %loadB
  %xor1 = xor i32 %sub2, %loadA
  %or1 = or i32 %xor1, %loadB
  %result = add i32 %sum, %or1
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %inc, 10
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret i32 %result
}

declare hidden i32 @single_use_helper(i32)

define i32 @inline_prevents_unroll(ptr %A) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %load = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %helper_result = call i32 @single_use_helper(i32 %load)
  %add = add i32 %sum, %helper_result
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %inc, 10
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret i32 %add
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.enable"}

define i32 @indirectbr_loop(ptr %A, ptr %target) {
entry:
  indirectbr ptr %target, [label %for.body, label %exit]

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %load = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %add = add i32 %sum, %load
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %inc, 10
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %result = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  ret i32 %result
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.enable"}

; Function Attrs: convergent
declare void @convergent_func() #0

; Function Attrs: convergent nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none)
declare token @llvm.experimental.convergence.anchor() #1

define i32 @extended_convergence(ptr %A, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %for.body, !llvm.loop !0

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  %tok = call token @llvm.experimental.convergence.anchor()
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %load = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %add = add i32 %sum, %load
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %inc, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  call void @convergent_func() [ "convergencectrl"(token %tok) ]
  ret i32 %add
}

attributes #0 = { convergent }
attributes #1 = { convergent nocallback nofree nosync nounwind willreturn memory(none) }

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.enable"}

; Function Attrs: noduplicate
declare void @noduplicate_func() #0

define i32 @noduplicate_prevents_unroll(ptr %A) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %load = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  call void @noduplicate_func()
  %add = add i32 %sum, %load
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %inc, 8
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret i32 %add
}

attributes #0 = { noduplicate }

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.enable"}

define i32 @full_unroll_cost_exceeds(ptr %A) {
entry:
  br label %for.body

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %load = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %add = add i32 %sum, %load
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %inc, 100
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body
  ret i32 %add
}

define i32 @pragma_full_unroll_unknown_tc(ptr %A, i32 %n) {
entry:
  %cmp.entry = icmp sgt i32 %n, 0
  br i1 %cmp.entry, label %for.body, label %exit

for.body:                                         ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %inc, %for.body ]
  %sum = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  %arrayidx = getelementptr inbounds i32, ptr %A, i32 %i
  %load = load i32, ptr %arrayidx, align 4
  %add = add i32 %sum, %load
  %inc = add i32 %i, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %inc, %n
  br i1 %cmp, label %for.body, label %exit, !llvm.loop !0

exit:                                             ; preds = %for.body, %entry
  %result = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %add, %for.body ]
  ret i32 %result
}

!0 = distinct !{!0, !1}
!1 = !{!"llvm.loop.unroll.full"}

comments.author: justinfargnoli
comments.body: Question: Are changes that solely affect `LLVM_DEBUG()` considered NFC?

comments.author: justinfargnoli
comments.body:
> Do you have an example of before/after output?

Hi @boomanaiden154, I carved out some tests from #178476's `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/debug-and-remarks.ll` and added them to `llvm/test/Transforms/LoopUnroll/debug.ll` to demonstrate the effect of this change.
